notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Viper, which the field rarely builds (0.5% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Crunch (it never builds Berserker's Axe, Salvation) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime, extended fights and physical survival: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +14.1% on your burst combo at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

metaBuilds.title: Physical Bruiser
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for magical survivability, online around minute 25 — about 4,664 effective HP vs magical in the sim.

metaBuilds.title: Physical Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 26 — about 729 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 3% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

metaBuilds.title: Physical DPS Carry
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 26 — about 199 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +66% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Augmentation (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their 3s all-in loses 23% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Basilisk: their 3s all-in loses 21% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their 3s all-in loses 27% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Basilisk: their 3s all-in loses 26% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Basilisk: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 20% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Combo Crunch” ⇒ on-hit; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field offlane 47.7% over 252,410 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 27% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 16% of its bite; you give up 27% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 27% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 27% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Kallari's main threat is Guillotine; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 27% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Rampage deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Augmentation: their 3s all-in loses 16% of its bite; you give up 27% of your damage.
